title: Intriguing Malt Ethiopian Teff Kik Alicha
description: A malty and light Ethiopian dish featuring teff and sansho, perfect for a midnight craving. This ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly recipe is made with double-rise-bread and served in a terracotta dish.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine teff flour, sansho pepper, and salt.
Gradually add water to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased terracotta dish and let it rise for 1 hour.
Bake the teff bread in the preheated oven for 30 minutes.
While the bread is baking, heat the vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the pan and saute until they are softened.
Once the bread is ready, let it cool for a few minutes before slicing it into small pieces.
In a separate pan, combine the malt syrup and 1 tablespoon of water, and bring to a boil over low heat.
Add the sliced bread to the pan and toss it with the malt syrup mixture until the bread is well coated.
